notableMoments: Qualified for the 2026 World Cup, their first ever appearance at the tournament — one of the greatest achievements in the history of small-nation football
With a population of approximately 600,000, Cabo Verde became one of the smallest nations ever to qualify for a World Cup, an extraordinary underdog story
The island nation off the west coast of Africa has built its squad from diaspora players scattered across European leagues, particularly in Portugal, France, and the Netherlands
Reached the quarterfinal of the 2023 Africa Cup of Nations, their best ever result, putting the football world on notice
Ryan Mendes and Garry Rodrigues are the iconic figures of Cabo Verdean football, with decades of faithful service to the national team between them
The team's qualification campaign was built on defensive solidity and team spirit, proving that collective heart can overcome individual talent disparities
Cabo Verde's football development has been a source of immense national pride, uniting diaspora communities around the world behind the Blue Sharks
The 2026 World Cup is the culmination of decades of steady progress, from being ranked outside the top 150 in world rankings to competing at the highest level
